summary: Verified iter245 voice-message feature in the public order-chat (customer ↔ driver). Backend: ALL 6 PYTEST CASES PASS (test_order_chat_voice_iter245.py): POST /api/order-chat/{order_id}/voice returns type='voice' with audio_url under /api/uploads/voice/ and duration; the audio_url is publicly fetchable (200 + video/webm content-type); GET /api/order-chat/{order_id} lists the uploaded voice message; text POST still works and coexists with voice; non-existent order returns 404; empty file returns 400. Frontend (PublicTracking /track/{orderId}): chat overlay (data-testid=track-chat-overlay) opens, the mic button (data-testid=track-voice-record-btn), text input (data-testid=track-chat-input), and send button (data-testid=track-chat-send) are all visible; 2 voice-bubble + 2 voice-play-btn render for the previously uploaded voice notes; text round-trip works end-to-end (typed 'TEST_UI_1781812760', sent via UI, rendered in chat panel and persisted in DB).
critical_code_review_comments: PublicTracking.jsx: chat button (track-chat-btn) is gated by `driver` being assigned to the order. Order 252ccd0a-… had no driver_id in seed data, so the button was initially absent — testing required a driver assignment. Consider rendering the chat button (disabled) even without a driver, OR add UX hint 'محادثة متاحة بعد تخصيص السائق' so users/QA understand.

VoiceChat.jsx VoiceRecordButton requires a real getUserMedia mic which cannot be exercised in headless automation — verifying the button + the playback bubble for existing voice messages is the agreed-upon coverage (per main agent note).

send_order_chat_voice correctly excludes _id from response (doc.pop('_id', None)). Good. UPLOAD_DIR/voice is created on demand; static mount at /api/uploads exposes files publicly — fine since order-chat is unauthenticated by design.
Voice size limit (5 MB) and content-type sniffing for ext (webm/ogg/mp4/mp3) look sensible. Consider also enforcing a max duration (server-side) as a defense-in-depth.
server.py is huge (~23.8k lines) — long-standing comment from prior iters; not in scope.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (mic btn, voice bubble, text round-trip all verified)

test_credentials: Public tracking is unauthenticated. Used order id 252ccd0a-e564-4a37-bb6c-974f8668169d. Test-time prerequisite: order needed driver_id assigned for the chat button to appear (assigned demo-drv-1 'سائق أحمد' to the order in DB).
seed_data_creation: Assigned driver demo-drv-1 (سائق أحمد / 07801111111) to order 252ccd0a-… so the chat UI surfaces. Uploaded 2 voice messages (.webm) and 2 text messages prefixed TEST_ to that order via the chat API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend voice chat is fully green: run `pytest backend/tests/test_order_chat_voice_iter245.py -v`. On the public tracking UI, the chat button only renders when the order has a driver assigned (see action_items / code-review item 1). For order 252ccd0a-… a driver has now been assigned in the DB and the chat overlay/bubbles are all confirmed working.
rca of the issue: Initial frontend test failed because the seeded order 252ccd0a-… had no driver_id; PublicTracking gates the chat button on `driver` truthy. Fix in test setup: assigned demo-drv-1 to the order via Mongo, after which all chat UI testids appeared and the text + voice flows verified end-to-end.
